name: ExtensionAppointment_Note
title: R5: note additional types
description: R5: `Appointment.note` additional types (Annotation) additional types from child elements (time)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Appointment.note` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Appointment.note` 0..* `Annotation`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Appointment.note` 0..* `Annotation`
*  R4B: `Appointment.comment` 0..1 `string`
*  R4: `Appointment.comment` 0..1 `string`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Appointment.note` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Appointment.note` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Appointment.comment` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Appointment.note` do not cover the following types: Annotation.
The mappings for `Appointment.note` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: time.

element.definition: R5: `Appointment.note` additional types (Annotation) additional types from child elements (time)
element.comment:
Element `Appointment.note` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Appointment.note` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Appointment.comment` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Appointment.note` do not cover the following types: Annotation.
The mappings for `Appointment.note` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: time.
Additional text to aid in facilitating the appointment. For instance, a note might be, "patient should proceed immediately to infusion room upon arrival"Where this is a planned appointment and the start/end dates are not set then this field can be used to provide additional guidance on the details of the appointment request, including any restrictions on when to book it.Typically only the concept.text will be used, however occasionally a reference to some generic documentation (or specific) and also supports coded instructions if/when they are required.
